title: DigitalOcean, Micron, And Rocket Lab Are Among Top 10 Large-Cap Gainers Last Week (May 4-May 8): Are the Others in Your Portfolio?
author: Nabaparna Bhattacharya
description: Wall Street's momentum trade accelerated last week as earnings beats and AI-fueled optimism drove massive rallies across tech and infrastructure stocks. Ten large-cap stocks emerged as top performers, with gains ranging from 32% to 57%, driven by better-than-expected earnings, raised guidance, and major partnerships in cloud computing, semiconductors, and space technology.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: 57.07% weekly gain driven by better-than-expected Q1 results and FY26 guidance raised above estimates
49.02% weekly gain following better-than-expected Q1 financial results
48.62% weekly gain after beating Q1 expectations and issuing Q2 guidance above estimates
57.07% weekly gain driven by better-than-expected Q1 results, raised FY26 guidance, Q2 guidance above estimates, and analyst price target increases
53.86% weekly gain following better-than-expected Q4 sales and guidance raises for Q1 and FY27
42.19% weekly gain after beating Q1 expectations and raising 2026 guidance above estimates
41.62% weekly gain driven by better-than-expected Q1 results, raised FY26 guidance, and $1.8 billion computing agreement with Anthropic
33.65% weekly gain as part of historic stretch with ~29% weekly performance, best weekly performance since December 2008
32.04% weekly gain following Nvidia partnership announcement for 5 gigawatts of AI infrastructure deployment and Nvidia's right to purchase up to 40 million shares
34.59% weekly gain after beating Q1 sales expectations, issuing above-estimate Q2 guidance, announcing Motiv Space Systems acquisition, and securing $30 million contract from Anduril Industries

title: Nasdaq 100 Climbs Records, Oil Extends Drop As Iran Weighs Hormuz Deal: Stock Market Today
author: Piero Cingari
description: U.S. tech stocks reached record highs on May 7, 2026, as crude oil tumbled nearly 3% on hopes Iran will accept a U.S. memorandum to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. The Nasdaq 100 gained 0.4% to 28,706 while the S&P 500 rose 0.2% to 7,376. Earnings drove significant stock-specific moves, with major winners including AAON (+36%), Datadog (+30%), and H&R Block (+26%), while notable losers included Planet Fitness (-33%), Insmed (-22%), and Zoetis (-22%).

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock surged 36% to record highs after Q1 results exceeded estimates and management flagged sharp acceleration in data-center cooling backlog.
Stock jumped 30% after Q1 revenue of $1.006B topped consensus and raised full-year guidance citing AI-related compute demand.
Stock climbed 26% after fiscal Q3 EPS of $6.02 crushed consensus and company raised FY26 EPS guidance and declared dividend.
Stock jumped 23% after Q1 EPS beat and company raised full-year revenue guidance.
Stock climbed 15% after Q1 results topped expectations and announced $1.3B North American joint venture expansion.
Stock tumbled 33% after lowering full-year EPS guidance and trimming revenue guidance below consensus.
Stock plunged 22% after reaffirming rather than raising sales target, signaling slowdown after blockbuster launch quarter.
Stock sank 22% after missing on both earnings and revenue lines, with management citing challenging market environment.
Stock cratered 38% despite Q1 EPS beat due to weak Q2 revenue and EPS guidance well below current quarter.
Stock dropped 29% on soft quarter and cautious commentary on store-level margins.
Stock rose 2.7% as part of broader tech strength driving Nasdaq to record highs.
Stock climbed 2.9% as part of Magnificent Seven strength driving market to records.
Stock gained 2.5% to fresh record near $213 as part of tech sector rally.
Stock popped 12% on Q1 EPS beat and bullish Q2 marketplace GOV guidance.
Stock dropped 17% despite 17% sales growth due to 12.3% decline in adjusted net income from margin compression.
Stock fell 10% after beating estimates but flagging supply constraints limiting datacenter CPU sales to $1B of $2B projected demand.

title: Russell 2000 Stocks: Too Early or Finally Interesting?
author: Chris Markoch
description: The Russell 2000 small-cap index trades at a significant valuation discount (19x P/E vs 27x for S&P 500) despite higher interest rates limiting growth for unprofitable companies. Analysts suggest now may be an opportune time to position in small-cap stocks, particularly those benefiting from infrastructure spending and domestic manufacturing. Two highlighted examples are Mueller Water Products and AAON Inc., both positioned to benefit from government investment and data center demand.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Positioned as an attractive entry point with significant valuation discount to large-caps; institutional buying-to-selling ratio of 4:1 indicates strong institutional interest; diversified sector exposure (financials, healthcare, industrials, consumer discretionary) offers growth potential if interest rates decline.

Up 16% in 2026 with analyst consensus suggesting 10%+ upside; benefits from federal water infrastructure investment; US-based manufacturing limits tariff exposure; solid revenue and earnings growth with P/E of 22 trading at discount to industrial peers.

Strong $1.3 billion backlog supporting 18-20% sales growth guidance; positioned to benefit from data center HVAC demand; US-based manufacturing aligns with onshoring trends; analyst price target of $107.75 implies ~30% upside despite recent volatility.

title: AAON Announces Quarterly Cash Dividend
author: Prnewswire
description: AAON, Inc. (NASDAQ:AAON) announced its Board of Directors has declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.10 per share ($0.40 annually), payable on March 30, 2026 to stockholders of record as of March 18, 2026. The company is a global leader in HVAC solutions for commercial, industrial and data center environments.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The announcement of a regular quarterly cash dividend demonstrates the company's financial health and commitment to returning value to shareholders. Consistent dividend payments are typically viewed positively by investors as they indicate stable earnings and management confidence in future performance.

title: Tesla's Daring Move
author: Motley Fool Staff
description: Tesla announced ambitious plans to double capital spending to $20 billion in 2026, investing heavily in autonomous vehicles, humanoid robots (Optimus), and AI infrastructure while discontinuing Model S and X production. The move signals a strategic pivot from traditional EV manufacturing toward autonomy and robotics, though analysts remain skeptical about timeline feasibility given Tesla's history of missed deadlines. Meanwhile, Meta surged 9% on strong ad revenue and AI investments despite doubling CapEx guidance, while Microsoft fell 12% as Azure growth slowed despite strong OpenAI-related backlog.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Tesla reported declining auto segment revenue (-11% Q4) and lowest Q4 earnings since 2020, but announced bold strategic pivot to robotics and autonomy with $20B capital spending. While the vision is ambitious, analysts express skepticism about timeline feasibility and whether cash reserves can sustain investments without additional capital raises. The move represents both opportunity and execution risk.

Meta beat revenue expectations and provided strong Q1 guidance, driving 9% stock surge. Despite doubling CapEx to $115-135B in 2026, investors rewarded the company for demonstrating clear returns on AI investments through improved ad targeting and revenue growth. Operating margins are improving, suggesting operational efficiency alongside aggressive spending.

Microsoft shares fell 12% after reporting Azure Cloud growth slowdown (39% vs 40% prior year) and higher-than-expected CapEx. While the company has strong OpenAI-related backlog (45% of RPO), investors expressed concern about ROI on massive capital expenditures. Stock trades at 30x earnings after 25% decline from highs, suggesting market skepticism about valuation and growth justification.

Alphabet is highlighted as the AI darling that has outperformed the Magnificent 7 over the past 12 months. The article notes sentiment shifted from viewing Alphabet as an AI laggard a year ago to now being the best performer, demonstrating successful AI strategy execution and market confidence.

Alphabet is highlighted as the AI darling that has outperformed the Magnificent 7 over the past 12 months. The article notes sentiment shifted from viewing Alphabet as an AI laggard a year ago to now being the best performer, demonstrating successful AI strategy execution and market confidence.

Southwest surged 15% after earnings, announcing strategic shift to profitability by ending free baggage and open seating policies. Guidance of $4 EPS (vs $3 analyst expectations) and strong balance sheet with minimal debt ($5B vs $25B market cap) position it favorably against competitors. P/E under 12 offers attractive valuation.

AAON is positioned as an attractive AI infrastructure play in data center cooling. Despite integration challenges from 2023 acquisition, backlog doubled year-over-year and operational efficiencies are materializing. Stock remains beaten down while industry tailwinds are strong, offering turnaround potential at reasonable valuation.

Badger Meter was crushed after earnings due to slower expected growth, but fundamentals remain strong with all-time high operating margins (~20%), pristine balance sheet, and 30+ year dividend history. Large projects like 1.6M meters in Puerto Rico provide growth catalysts. Stock offers long-term stability with attractive entry point after selloff.
